VEILCORP ARCHIVES // CORPORATE ANOMALY REPORT // CA-001

Classification: False Steward
Threat Level: Escalating

INITIAL BEHAVIOR

The host was once considered resistant to predatory digital phenomena. It distributed quality games, respected physical ownership, and treated subscriptions as optional benefits rather than mandatory life support.

Operators regarded the host as trustworthy.

OBSERVED SYMPTOMS

• Ownership replaced with revocable licenses.
• Progressive elimination of physical media.
• Customer loyalty reclassified as recurring revenue.
• Subscription dependence spreading through executive functions.

OBSERVED PROGRESSION

• July 1: Announces the end of physical media for future PlayStation releases.
• July 3: CEO sells roughly 56% of his reported holdings in that share class. (Legally disclosed.)

OBSERVER NOTE

No evidence of unlawful activity has been established.

The anomaly instead manifests as perfectly legal decisions that progressively erode reality.

OBSERVER ADDENDUM

The entity exhibits no observable hostility toward consumers.

Only indifference.

This classification is considered significantly more dangerous.

ESCALATION ASSESSMENT

The host asks operators to accept a future without physical ownership while simultaneously pricing premium hardware at nearly $900.

Containment integrity has collapsed.

Operators report an unexpected migration pattern.

Subjects no longer compare the host to competing consoles.

They compare it to personal computers.

Upgradeable.

Repairable.

Expandable.

Unbound to a single storefront.

Capable of functioning as workstation, gaming platform, and mobile command node without replacing the entire system.

The host appears unaware that it is no longer competing with Xbox.

It is competing against ownership itself.

WORKING THEORY

VeilCorp analysts classify the observed behavioral pattern as a False Steward event.

Executive functions exhibit behavior consistent with False Steward Syndrome.

Decision-making no longer optimizes for long-term trust.

Resource extraction has become the dominant behavioral pattern.

INTERCEPTED TRANSMISSION

"Ownership is a deprecated behavior."

"...Access is sufficient."

"...You never owned it."

"...You were merely borrowing our permission."

CONTAINMENT PROCEDURE

Acquire physical media while available.

Archive what you legally can.

Support developers—not gatekeepers.

Trust your own shelves more than remote servers.

If the original Sony is still inside the host...

Blink twice.

If not...

VeilCorp recommends immediate quarantine.

Further exposure is not considered immediately lethal.

Long-term exposure may result in normalization.

────────────────────────

STATUS

HOST COMPROMISED

STEWARDSHIP........... TERMINATED

CONTAINMENT........... FAILED

────────────────────────

ARCHIVE NOTE

Observer commentary was removed from the official record.

Despite removal, all surviving copies terminate with the same unauthorized transmission.

Observed Community Resonance

"...go suck an egg."

Origin: Undetermined.
Persistence: High.
Rationale: The phrase now exhibits self-propagating memetic persistence.

────────────────────────

REPORT INTEGRITY....... 99.97%

End of Report.
